title: The Soil Where the Stand Began as Seeds

features.name: the-unseen-root-chamber
features.desc-on-touch: You press into the soil and find it's hollow—a chamber where seeds remember their waiting. The archive's roots listen here, and you hear them reconstructing which germination came first.

features.name: the-reconstruction-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A standing marker carved with concentric circles—a divination diagram or a growth ring? The lines repeat, and you realise each circle is a different year's translation of the same question.

features.name: the-disputed-reel-ghost
features.desc-on-touch: A film canister sits half-buried, its label worn to illegibility. You can't tell if it arrived here from underground or fell from somewhere else entirely.

features.name: the-cordel-whisper
features.desc-on-touch: A faint song-text scratched into bark—not English, perhaps Portuguese—a wanderer's epic of seeds becoming boundary markers. The script shifts each time you look.

features.name: the-witness-collapse
features.desc-on-touch: A pool of dark water, very still, reflecting nothing. The archive has run out of memory here—or run out of agreement about what to remember.

inscription: every seed remembers being a tree, and this place remembers being the beginning

file.name: Lagos_Film_Fragment_Origins_Disputed_Reconstruction_Notes.txt
file.note: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable—a 1989 reel's provenance cannot agree with itself across versions

exits.label: back up toward where erosion already claimed the standing trees
deeper down to where seeds have learned to listen to the world below them
toward the overstory, where the canopy is still learning what the soil knew first
